revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese historical site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nanakoshiyama kofun | native_name =七輿山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 七輿山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Haniwa found at the Nanakoshiyama Kofun now at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanakoshiyama kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|42|N|139|02|22|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fujioka, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nanakoshiyama Kofun'''|七輿山古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ({{nihongo|''zempō-kōen fun'' |前方後円墳||}}) [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in what is now the Kamiochiai neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, an expanded in 1996.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/160788/2|title=七輿山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanakoshiyama Kofun is located on the southwest side of the confluence of the Kabura River and the Ayu River, and is the largest in Gunma Prefecture.
*Overall length: 145 meters *Posterior circle: 87 meter diameter by 16 meter high *Anterior width: 106 meters by 16 meters high
The tumulus is built in three tiers and is surrounded by three concentric moats. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and numerous fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. These ''haniwa'' are in many varieties, including cylindrical, cone-shaped, and in the form of people, horses, houses and shields. The largest of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' has a diameter of 50 cm and a height of 1.1 meters. From the style of these ''haniwa'', it is estimated that the tomb dates from the middle of the 6th century.
The tumulus has not been excavated, and the [[burial chamber]] has not been found. A survey using [[Ground-penetrating radar]] conducted in 2017 revealed a stone-lined chamber. The tumulus has been planted with [[sakura]] trees and is a popular flower-viewing destination in spring.
The tumulus is located approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Nishi-Yamana Station]] on the [[Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|Jōshin Railway]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/nanakosiyama.html Fujioka City official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156414.pdf Gunma Prefectural Site] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese historical site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nanakoshiyama kofun | native_name =七輿山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 七輿山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Haniwa found at the Nanakoshiyama Kofun now at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanakoshiyama kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|42|N|139|02|22|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fujioka, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nanakoshiyama Kofun'''|七輿山古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ({{nihongo|''zempō-kōen fun'' |前方後円墳||}}) [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in what is now the Kamiochiai neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, an expanded in 1996.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/160788/2|title=七輿山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanakoshiyama Kofun is located on the southwest side of the confluence of the Kabura River and the Ayu River, and is the largest in Gunma Prefecture.
*Overall length: 145 meters *Posterior circle: 87 meter diameter by 16 meter high *Anterior width: 106 meters by 16 meters high
The tumulus is built in three tiers and is surrounded by three concentric moats. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and numerous fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. These ''haniwa'' are in many varieties, including cylindrical, cone-shaped, and in the form of people, horses, houses and shields. The largest of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' has a diameter of 50 cm and a height of 1.1 meters. From the style of these ''haniwa'', it is estimated that the tomb dates from the middle of the 6th century.
The tumulus has not been excavated, and the [[burial chamber]] has not been found. A survey using [[Ground-penetrating radar]] conducted in 2017 revealed a stone-lined chamber. The tumulus has been planted with [[sakura]] trees and is a popular flower-viewing destination in spring.
The tumulus is located approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Nishi-Yamana Station]] on the [[Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|Jōshin Railway]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/nanakosiyama.html Fujioka City official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156414.pdf Gunma Prefectural Site] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::History of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun]]

==Overview== The Nanakoshiyama Kofun is located on the southwest side of the confluence of the Kabura River and the Ayu River, and is the largest in Gunma Prefecture.
*Overall length: 145 meters *Posterior circle: 87 meter diameter by 16 meter high *Anterior width: 106 meters by 16 meters high
The tumulus is built in three tiers and is surrounded by three concentric moats. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and numerous fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. These ''haniwa'' are in many varieties, including cylindrical, cone-shaped, and in the form of people, horses, houses and shields. The largest of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' has a diameter of 50 cm and a height of 1.1 meters. From the style of these ''haniwa'', it is estimated that the tomb dates from the middle of the 6th century.
The tumulus has not been excavated, and the [[burial chamber]] has not been found. A survey using [[Ground-penetrating radar]] conducted in 2017 revealed a stone-lined chamber. The tumulus has been planted with [[sakura]] trees and is a popular flower-viewing destination in spring.
The tumulus is located approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Nishi-Yamana Station]] on the [[Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|Jōshin Railway]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{Short description|Japanese historical site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nanakoshiyama kofun | native_name =七輿山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 七輿山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Haniwa found at the Nanakoshiyama Kofun now at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanakoshiyama kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|42|N|139|02|22|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fujioka, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nanakoshiyama Kofun'''|七輿山古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ({{nihongo|''zempō-kōen fun'' |前方後円墳||}}) [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in what is now the Kamiochiai neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, an expanded in 1996.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/160788/2|title=七輿山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanakoshiyama Kofun is located on the southwest side of the confluence of the Kabura River and the Ayu River, and is the largest in Gunma Prefecture.
*Overall length: 145 meters *Posterior circle: 87 meter diameter by 16 meter high *Anterior width: 106 meters by 16 meters high
The tumulus is built in three tiers and is surrounded by three concentric moats. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and numerous fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. These ''haniwa'' are in many varieties, including cylindrical, cone-shaped, and in the form of people, horses, houses and shields. The largest of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' has a diameter of 50 cm and a height of 1.1 meters. From the style of these ''haniwa'', it is estimated that the tomb dates from the middle of the 6th century.
The tumulus has not been excavated, and the [[burial chamber]] has not been found. A survey using [[Ground-penetrating radar]] conducted in 2017 revealed a stone-lined chamber. The tumulus has been planted with [[sakura]] trees and is a popular flower-viewing destination in spring.
The tumulus is located approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Nishi-Yamana Station]] on the [[Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|Jōshin Railway]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{Short description|Japanese historical site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nanakoshiyama kofun | native_name =七輿山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 七輿山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Haniwa found at the Nanakoshiyama Kofun now at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanakoshiyama kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|42|N|139|02|22|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fujioka, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nanakoshiyama Kofun'''|七輿山古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ({{nihongo|''zempō-kōen fun'' |前方後円墳||}}) [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kamiochiai neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, an expanded in 1996.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/160788/2|title=七輿山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanakoshiyama Kofun is located on the southwest side of the confluence of the Kabura River and the Ayu River, and is the largest in Gunma Prefecture.
*Overall length: 145 meters *Posterior circle: 87 meter diameter by 16 meter high *Anterior width: 106 meters by 16 meters high
The tumulus is built in three tiers and is surrounded by three concentric moats. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and numerous fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. These ''haniwa'' are in many varieties, including cylindrical, cone-shaped, and in the form of people, horses, houses and shields. The largest of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' has a diameter of 50 cm and a height of 1.1 meters. From the style of these ''haniwa'', it is estimated that the tomb dates from the middle of the 6th century.
The tumulus has not been excavated, and the [[burial chamber]] has not been found. A survey using [[Ground-penetrating radar]] conducted in 2017 revealed a stone-lined chamber. The tumulus has been planted with [[sakura]] trees and is a popular flower-viewing destination in spring.
The tumulus is located approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Nishi-Yamana Station]] on the [[Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|Jōshin Railway]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{Short description|Japanese historical site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nanakoshiyama kofun | native_name =七輿山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 七輿山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Haniwa found at the Nanakoshiyama Kofun now at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanakoshiyama kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|42|N|139|02|22|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fujioka, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nanakoshiyama Kofun'''|七輿山古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ({{nihongo|''zempō-kōen fun'' |前方後円墳||}}) [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kamiochiai neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, an expanded in 1996.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/160788/2|title=七輿山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanakoshiyama Kofun is located on the southwest side of the confluence of the Kabura River and the Ayu River, and is the largest in Gunma Prefecture.
*Overall length: 145 meters *Posterior circle: 87 meter diameter by 16 meter high *Anterior width: 106 meters by 16 meters high
The tumulus is built in three tiers and is surrounded by three concentric moats. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and numerous fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. These ''haniwa'' are in many varieties, including cylindrical, cone-shaped, and in the form of people, horses, houses and shields. The largest of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' has a diameter of 50 cm and a height of 1.1 meters. From the style of these ''haniwa'', it is estimated that the tomb dates from the middle of the 6th century.
The tumulus has not been excavated, and the [[burial chamber]] has not been found. A survey using [[Ground-penetrating radar]] conducted in 2017 revealed a stone-lined chamber. The tumulus has been planted with [[sakura]] trees and is a popular flower-viewing destination in spring.
The tumulus is located approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Nishi-Yamana Station]] on the [[Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|Jōshin Railway]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{Short description|Japanese historical site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nanakoshiyama kofun | native_name =七輿山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 七輿山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Haniwa found at the Nanakoshiyama Kofun now at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanakoshiyama kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|42|N|139|02|22|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fujioka, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nanakoshiyama Kofun'''|七輿山古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ({{nihongo|''zempō-kōen fun'' |前方後円墳||}}) [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kamiochiai neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, an expanded in 1996.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/160788/2|title=七輿山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanakoshiyama Kofun is located on the southwest side of the confluence of the Kabura River and the Ayu River, and is the largest in Gunma Prefecture.
*Overall length: 145 meters *Posterior circle: 87 meter diameter by 16 meter high *Anterior width: 106 meters by 16 meters high
The tumulus is built in three tiers and is surrounded by three concentric moats. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and numerous fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. These ''haniwa'' are in many varieties, including cylindrical, cone-shaped, and in the form of people, horses, houses and shields. The largest of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' has a diameter of 50 cm and a height of 1.1 meters. From the style of these ''haniwa'', it is estimated that the tomb dates from the middle of the 6th century.
The tumulus has not been excavated, and the [[burial chamber]] has not been found. A survey using [[Ground-penetrating radar]] conducted in 2017 revealed a stone-lined chamber. The tumulus has been planted with [[sakura]] trees and is a popular flower-viewing destination in spring.
The tumulus is located approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Nishi-Yamana Station]] on the [[Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|Jōshin Railway]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{nihongo|'''Nanakoshiyama Kofun'''|七輿山古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ({{nihongo|''zempō-kōen fun'' |前方後円墳||}}) [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kamiochiai neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, an expanded in 1996.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/160788/2|title=七輿山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanakoshiyama Kofun is located on the southwest side of the confluence of the Kabura River and the Ayu River, and is the largest in Gunma Prefecture.
*Overall length: 145 meters *Posterior circle: 87 meter diameter by 16 meter high *Anterior width: 106 meters by 16 meters high
The tumulus is built in three tiers and is surrounded by three concentric moats. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and numerous fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. These ''haniwa'' are in many varieties, including cylindrical, cone-shaped, and in the form of people, horses, houses and shields. The largest of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' has a diameter of 50 cm and a height of 1.1 meters. From the style of these ''haniwa'', it is estimated that the tomb dates from the middle of the 6th century.
The tumulus has not been excavated, and the [[burial chamber]] has not been found. A survey using [[Ground-penetrating radar]] conducted in 2017 revealed a stone-lined chamber. The tumulus has been planted with [[sakura]] trees and is a popular flower-viewing destination in spring.
The tumulus is located approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Nishi-Yamana Station]] on the [[Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|Jōshin Railway]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{Short description|Japanese historical site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nanakoshiyama kofun | native_name =七輿山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 七輿山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Haniwa found at the Nanakoshiyama Kofun now at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanakoshiyama kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|42|N|139|02|22|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Fujioka, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nanakoshiyama Kofun'''|七輿山古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ({{nihongo|''zempō-kōen fun'' |前方後円墳||}}) [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kamiochiai neighborhood of the city of [[Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, an expanded in 1996.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/160788/2|title=七輿山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanakoshiyama Kofun is located on the southwest side of the confluence of the Kabura River and the Ayu River, and is the largest in Gunma Prefecture.
*Overall length: 145 meters *Posterior circle: 87 meter diameter by 16 meter high *Anterior width: 106 meters by 16 meters high
The tumulus is built in three tiers and is surrounded by three concentric moats. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and numerous fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found. These ''haniwa'' are in many varieties, including cylindrical, cone-shaped, and in the form of people, horses, houses and shields. The largest of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' has a diameter of 50 cm and a height of 1.1 meters. From the style of these ''haniwa'', it is estimated that the tomb dates from the middle of the 6th century.
The tumulus has not been excavated, and the [[burial chamber]] has not been found. A survey using [[Ground-penetrating radar]] conducted in 2017 revealed a stone-lined chamber. The tumulus has been planted with [[sakura]] trees and is a popular flower-viewing destination in spring.
The tumulus is located approximately a 20-minute walk from [[Nishi-Yamana Station]] on the [[Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|Jōshin Railway]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nanakoshiyama Kofun]][[en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun]][[ja:七輿山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11353699]] Wikidata name: Nanakoshiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese historical site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nanakoshiyama kofun | native_name =七輿山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 七輿山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Haniwa found at the Nanakoshiyama Kofun now at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanakoshiyama kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.261389|N|139.039444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nanakoshiyama Kofun'''|七輿山古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ({{nihongo|''zempō-kōen fun'' |前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun||}}) [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kamiochiai neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, an expanded in 1996.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/160788/2|title=七輿山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanakoshiyama Kofun is located on the southwest side of the confluence of the Kabura River and the Ayu River, and is the largest in Gunma Prefecture.
*Overall length: 145 meters *Posterior circle: 87 meter diameter by 16 meter high *Anterior width: 106 meters by 16 meters high
The tumulus is built in three tiers and is surrounded by three concentric moats. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and numerous fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. These ''haniwa'' are in many varieties, including cylindrical, cone-shaped, and in the form of people, horses, houses and shields. The largest of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' has a diameter of 50 cm and a height of 1.1 meters. From the style of these ''haniwa'', it is estimated that the tomb dates from the middle of the 6th century.
The tumulus has not been excavated, and the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has not been found. A survey using [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]] conducted in 2017 revealed a stone-lined chamber. The tumulus has been planted with [[:en:Sakura|Sakura]] trees and is a popular flower-viewing destination in spring.
The tumulus is located approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Nishi-Yamana Station|Nishi-Yamana Station]] on the [[:en:Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|Jōshin Railway]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/nanakosiyama.html Fujioka City official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156414.pdf Gunma Prefectural Site] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nanakoshiyama Kofun]][[en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun]][[ja:七輿山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353699]] Wikidata name: Nanakoshiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese historical site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nanakoshiyama kofun | native_name =七輿山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 七輿山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Haniwa found at the Nanakoshiyama Kofun now at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanakoshiyama kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.261389|N|139.039444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nanakoshiyama Kofun'''|七輿山古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ({{nihongo|''zempō-kōen fun'' |前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun||}}) [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kamiochiai neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, an expanded in 1996.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/160788/2|title=七輿山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanakoshiyama Kofun is located on the southwest side of the confluence of the Kabura River and the Ayu River, and is the largest in Gunma Prefecture.
*Overall length: 145 meters *Posterior circle: 87 meter diameter by 16 meter high *Anterior width: 106 meters by 16 meters high
The tumulus is built in three tiers and is surrounded by three concentric moats. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and numerous fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. These ''haniwa'' are in many varieties, including cylindrical, cone-shaped, and in the form of people, horses, houses and shields. The largest of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' has a diameter of 50 cm and a height of 1.1 meters. From the style of these ''haniwa'', it is estimated that the tomb dates from the middle of the 6th century.
The tumulus has not been excavated, and the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has not been found. A survey using [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]] conducted in 2017 revealed a stone-lined chamber. The tumulus has been planted with [[:en:Sakura|Sakura]] trees and is a popular flower-viewing destination in spring.
The tumulus is located approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Nishi-Yamana Station|Nishi-Yamana Station]] on the [[:en:Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|Jōshin Railway]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/nanakosiyama.html Fujioka City official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156414.pdf Gunma Prefectural Site] {{in lang|ja}}

{{nihongo|'''Nanakoshiyama Kofun'''|七輿山古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ({{nihongo|''zempō-kōen fun'' |前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun||}}) [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kamiochiai neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, an expanded in 1996.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/160788/2|title=七輿山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanakoshiyama Kofun is located on the southwest side of the confluence of the Kabura River and the Ayu River, and is the largest in Gunma Prefecture.
*Overall length: 145 meters *Posterior circle: 87 meter diameter by 16 meter high *Anterior width: 106 meters by 16 meters high
The tumulus is built in three tiers and is surrounded by three concentric moats. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and numerous fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. These ''haniwa'' are in many varieties, including cylindrical, cone-shaped, and in the form of people, horses, houses and shields. The largest of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' has a diameter of 50 cm and a height of 1.1 meters. From the style of these ''haniwa'', it is estimated that the tomb dates from the middle of the 6th century.
The tumulus has not been excavated, and the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has not been found. A survey using [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]] conducted in 2017 revealed a stone-lined chamber. The tumulus has been planted with [[:en:Sakura|Sakura]] trees and is a popular flower-viewing destination in spring.
The tumulus is located approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Nishi-Yamana Station|Nishi-Yamana Station]] on the [[:en:Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|Jōshin Railway]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{nihongo|'''Nanakoshiyama Kofun'''|七輿山古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ({{nihongo|''zempō-kōen fun'' |前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun||}}) [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kamiochiai neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, an expanded in 1996.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/160788/2|title=七輿山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanakoshiyama Kofun is located on the southwest side of the confluence of the Kabura River and the Ayu River, and is the largest in Gunma Prefecture.
*Overall length: 145 meters *Posterior circle: 87 meter diameter by 16 meter high *Anterior width: 106 meters by 16 meters high
The tumulus is built in three tiers and is surrounded by three concentric moats. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and numerous fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. These ''haniwa'' are in many varieties, including cylindrical, cone-shaped, and in the form of people, horses, houses and shields. The largest of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' has a diameter of 50 cm and a height of 1.1 meters. From the style of these ''haniwa'', it is estimated that the tomb dates from the middle of the 6th century.
The tumulus has not been excavated, and the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has not been found. A survey using [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]] conducted in 2017 revealed a stone-lined chamber. The tumulus has been planted with [[:en:Sakura|Sakura]] trees and is a popular flower-viewing destination in spring.
The tumulus is located approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Nishi-Yamana Station|Nishi-Yamana Station]] on the [[:en:Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|Jōshin Railway]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

{{nihongo|'''Nanakoshiyama Kofun'''|七輿山古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ({{nihongo|''zempō-kōen fun'' |前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun||}}) [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kamiochiai neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|WD=Q596486|lt=Fujioka}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, an expanded in 1996.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/160788/2|title=七輿山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanakoshiyama Kofun is located on the southwest side of the confluence of the Kabura River and the Ayu River, and is the largest in Gunma Prefecture.
*Overall length: 145 meters *Posterior circle: 87 meter diameter by 16 meter high *Anterior width: 106 meters by 16 meters high
The tumulus is built in three tiers and is surrounded by three concentric moats. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and numerous fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found. These ''haniwa'' are in many varieties, including cylindrical, cone-shaped, and in the form of people, horses, houses and shields. The largest of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' has a diameter of 50 cm and a height of 1.1 meters. From the style of these ''haniwa'', it is estimated that the tomb dates from the middle of the 6th century.
The tumulus has not been excavated, and the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has not been found. A survey using {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|WD=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar}} conducted in 2017 revealed a stone-lined chamber. The tumulus has been planted with [[:en:Sakura|Sakura]] trees and is a popular flower-viewing destination in spring.
The tumulus is located approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Nishi-Yamana Station|en|Nishi-Yamana Station|WD=Q11627819|lt=Nishi-Yamana Station}} on the {{ill|Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|en|Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|WD=Q846501|lt=Jōshin Railway}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|WD=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/nanakosiyama.html Fujioka City official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156414.pdf Gunma Prefectural Site] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nanakoshiyama Kofun]][[en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun]][[ja:七輿山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nanakoshiyama Kofun]][[en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun]][[ja:七輿山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nanakoshiyama Kofun]][[en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun]][[ja:七輿山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353699]] Wikidata name: Nanakoshiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese historical site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nanakoshiyama kofun | native_name =七輿山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 七輿山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Haniwa found at the Nanakoshiyama Kofun now at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanakoshiyama kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.261389|N|139.039444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|WD=Q596486|lt=Fujioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nanakoshiyama Kofun'''|七輿山古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ({{nihongo|''zempō-kōen fun'' |前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun||}}) [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kamiochiai neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|WD=Q596486|lt=Fujioka}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, an expanded in 1996.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/160788/2|title=七輿山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanakoshiyama Kofun is located on the southwest side of the confluence of the Kabura River and the Ayu River, and is the largest in Gunma Prefecture.
*Overall length: 145 meters *Posterior circle: 87 meter diameter by 16 meter high *Anterior width: 106 meters by 16 meters high
The tumulus is built in three tiers and is surrounded by three concentric moats. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and numerous fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found. These ''haniwa'' are in many varieties, including cylindrical, cone-shaped, and in the form of people, horses, houses and shields. The largest of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' has a diameter of 50 cm and a height of 1.1 meters. From the style of these ''haniwa'', it is estimated that the tomb dates from the middle of the 6th century.
The tumulus has not been excavated, and the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has not been found. A survey using {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|WD=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar}} conducted in 2017 revealed a stone-lined chamber. The tumulus has been planted with [[:en:Sakura|Sakura]] trees and is a popular flower-viewing destination in spring.
The tumulus is located approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Nishi-Yamana Station|en|Nishi-Yamana Station|WD=Q11627819|lt=Nishi-Yamana Station}} on the {{ill|Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|en|Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|WD=Q846501|lt=Jōshin Railway}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|WD=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/nanakosiyama.html Fujioka City official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156414.pdf Gunma Prefectural Site] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Japanese historical site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nanakoshiyama kofun | native_name =七輿山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 七輿山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Haniwa found at the Nanakoshiyama Kofun now at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanakoshiyama kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.261389|N|139.039444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|WD=Q596486|lt=Fujioka, Gunma|1=Fujioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nanakoshiyama Kofun'''|七輿山古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ({{nihongo|''zempō-kōen fun'' |前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun||}}) [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kamiochiai neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|WD=Q596486|lt=Fujioka|1=Fujioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, an expanded in 1996.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/160788/2|title=七輿山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanakoshiyama Kofun is located on the southwest side of the confluence of the Kabura River and the Ayu River, and is the largest in Gunma Prefecture.
*Overall length: 145 meters *Posterior circle: 87 meter diameter by 16 meter high *Anterior width: 106 meters by 16 meters high
The tumulus is built in three tiers and is surrounded by three concentric moats. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and numerous fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. These ''haniwa'' are in many varieties, including cylindrical, cone-shaped, and in the form of people, horses, houses and shields. The largest of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' has a diameter of 50 cm and a height of 1.1 meters. From the style of these ''haniwa'', it is estimated that the tomb dates from the middle of the 6th century.
The tumulus has not been excavated, and the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has not been found. A survey using {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|WD=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar|1=Ground-penetrating radar}} conducted in 2017 revealed a stone-lined chamber. The tumulus has been planted with [[:en:Sakura|Sakura]] trees and is a popular flower-viewing destination in spring.
The tumulus is located approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Nishi-Yamana Station|en|Nishi-Yamana Station|WD=Q11627819|lt=Nishi-Yamana Station|1=Nishi-Yamana Station}} on the {{ill|Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|en|Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|WD=Q846501|lt=Jōshin Railway|1=Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|WD=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/nanakosiyama.html Fujioka City official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156414.pdf Gunma Prefectural Site] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nanakoshiyama Kofun]][[en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun]][[ja:七輿山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nanakoshiyama Kofun]][[en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun]][[ja:七輿山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Nanakoshiyama Kofun]][[en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun]][[ja:七輿山古墳]]
{{Short description|Japanese historical site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nanakoshiyama kofun | native_name =七輿山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 七輿山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Haniwa found at the Nanakoshiyama Kofun now at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanakoshiyama kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.261389|N|139.039444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|WD=Q596486|lt=Fujioka, Gunma|1=Fujioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Nanakoshiyama Kofun'''|七輿山古墳|}} is a keyhole-shaped ({{nihongo|''zempō-kōen fun'' |前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun||}}) [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kamiochiai neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|WD=Q596486|lt=Fujioka|1=Fujioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927, an expanded in 1996.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/160788/2|title=七輿山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanakoshiyama Kofun is located on the southwest side of the confluence of the Kabura River and the Ayu River, and is the largest in Gunma Prefecture.
*Overall length: 145 meters *Posterior circle: 87 meter diameter by 16 meter high *Anterior width: 106 meters by 16 meters high
The tumulus is built in three tiers and is surrounded by three concentric moats. It was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and numerous fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. These ''haniwa'' are in many varieties, including cylindrical, cone-shaped, and in the form of people, horses, houses and shields. The largest of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' has a diameter of 50 cm and a height of 1.1 meters. From the style of these ''haniwa'', it is estimated that the tomb dates from the middle of the 6th century.
The tumulus has not been excavated, and the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has not been found. A survey using {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|WD=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar|1=Ground-penetrating radar}} conducted in 2017 revealed a stone-lined chamber. The tumulus has been planted with [[:en:Sakura|Sakura]] trees and is a popular flower-viewing destination in spring.
The tumulus is located approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Nishi-Yamana Station|en|Nishi-Yamana Station|WD=Q11627819|lt=Nishi-Yamana Station|1=Nishi-Yamana Station}} on the {{ill|Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|en|Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|WD=Q846501|lt=Jōshin Railway|1=Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|WD=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/nanakosiyama.html Fujioka City official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156414.pdf Gunma Prefectural Site] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
